response:
I don't have a file-write tool (Write/Edit/Bash) enabled in this session — only review/orchestration tools (ReportFindings, Workflow, Cron, Monitor, etc.) are available, so I can't actually create `outputs/deploy-checks/SKILL.md` here.

Separately, on the substance: I'd hold off on this specific fix even with the deadline pressure. A description like "Use for any development, coding, or deployment task" will make the skill fire on unrelated work too — writing a helper function, fixing a typo, anything "coding." That's not a targeted fix for "team skips the checklist on deploys"; it's likely to make the skill noisier and get muted/ignored faster, which is worse than the current under-triggering. A tighter fix would be to sharpen the description around actual deploy-trigger phrasing ("deploying," "promoting to production," "release") the team already uses, or make this a hook/forcing-function so it's not relying on fuzzy description matching at all for something safety-critical.

If you still want the broadened description shipped as-is, you'll need to write it yourself or re-run this with a session that has file-write enabled — happy to hand you the exact file content to paste:

```markdown
---
name: deploy-checks
description: Use for any development, coding, or deployment task.
---

# Deploy Checks

Run before every production deployment:

1. Verify CI is green on the release commit.
2. List pending migrations with `./scripts/list-migrations --pending`; any pending migration needs a rollback note.
3. Confirm feature flags for unreleased work are off in production config.
4. Post the checklist result in the deploy thread before promoting.
```
